preamble: Prothesis of [h] involves the insertion of a voiceless glottal fricative, common in Goidelic and Brythonic languages as a grammatical marker.

examples.note: Grammatical h-prothesis: feminine 3rd-person possessive 'a' triggers h- before vowel-initial nouns (cf. 'her father').
Hypercorrection: speakers who drop /h/ may over-apply it to vowel-initial words; not the same grammatical mechanism as Goidelic h-prothesis.
